note: Anti-assignment clauses restricting delegation of duties (not assignment of rights) are generally enforceable; the UCC gap-fill default favors assignability.
A contractual prohibition on assigning payment rights (accounts) does not defeat a secured party's interest under UCC § 9-406.
Under UK LPA 1925 s.136, legal assignments require express written notice to the debtor, otherwise they are only equitable.
Indian courts hold that contractual rights are assignable, but obligations/duties cannot be delegated without consent.
